info.title: Tandemn vLLM Server API
info.description:
API for distributed Large Language Model inference using the Tandemn vLLM server.
This endpoint provides OpenAI-compatible chat completions with support for streaming responses.

post.description:
Creates a chat completion for the provided messages using the specified model.
This endpoint is compatible with OpenAI's chat completion API format and supports
streaming responses via Server-Sent Events (SSE).

messages.content: What is the capital of France?

200.description: Successful response - returns a stream of chat completion chunks

Cache-Control.description: Prevents caching of the streaming response

Connection.description: Keeps the connection alive for streaming

404.description: Model not found or not ready

value.detail: No deployment found for model llama-3.2-1b
Deployment for model llama-3.2-1b is not ready

model.description:
The name of the deployed model to use for the chat completion.
Must match a model that has been deployed via the /deploy endpoint.

stream.description:
Whether to stream the response using Server-Sent Events (SSE).
When true, returns partial message deltas as they are generated.

messages.description:
The messages to generate a chat completion for.
Supports roles: "system", "user", "assistant", "tool" (developer role is converted to system).

role.description: The role of the message author

max_completion_tokens.description:
The maximum number of tokens to generate in the completion.
Maps to vLLM's max_tokens parameter.

temperature.description:
Sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2.
Higher values make the output more random, lower values more deterministic.

top_p.description:
Nucleus sampling parameter. The model considers tokens with top_p probability mass.
E.g., 0.1 means only tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered.

top_k.description: The number of highest probability vocabulary tokens to keep for top-k filtering.

min_p.description: Minimum probability for a token to be considered, relative to the most likely token.

min_tokens.description: The minimum number of tokens to generate before stopping.

frequency_penalty.description:
Penalizes tokens based on their frequency in the generated text so far.
Positive values decrease likelihood of repetition.

repetition_penalty.description:
Penalizes tokens that have already appeared in the generated text.
Values > 1.0 discourage repetition, < 1.0 encourage it.

presence_penalty.description:
Penalizes tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far.
Positive values encourage the model to talk about new topics.

n.description: Number of chat completion choices to generate. Currently hardcoded to 1.

eos_token_id.description:
List of token IDs that should trigger the end of generation.
Maps to vLLM's stop_token_ids parameter.

stop.description: List of strings that should trigger the end of generation when encountered.

ChatCompletionStreamResponse.description:
Server-Sent Event stream format for chat completion chunks.
Each chunk is prefixed with "data: " and followed by two newlines.
The stream ends with "data: [DONE]".

id.description: Unique identifier for this chat completion

created.description: Unix timestamp when this chunk was created

model.description: The model used for this completion

choices.description: Array of completion choices (currently always 1 choice)

finish_reason.description: Reason the generation finished (only in final chunk)

detail.example: No deployment found for model llama-3.2-1b

BearerAuth.description:
Bearer token authentication (currently not enforced).
Reserved for future implementation.

tags.name: Chat Completions
tags.description:
OpenAI-compatible chat completion endpoints for generating responses from deployed language models.
Supports streaming responses and various sampling parameters for fine-tuning generation behavior.
